title: baltimora recta var. genuina
intro: Baltimora recta var. genuina, also known as the common balm or sweet balm, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Baltimora recta var. genuina include reducing fever, relieving digestive problems, and soothing respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as teas, infusions, or used topically in the form of ointments, salves, or creams to harness its medicinal properties.
